## Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in E.D.N.Y.: 13,187 Cases, 96.4% Dismissal Rate -- Open Bankruptcy Project
Source: https://openbankruptcyproject.org/news/chapter-13-e-d-n-y-bankruptcy-data.html

Open Bankruptcy Project

News
Research
About
Contact

KANSAS CITY, April 3, 2026 --

Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in E.D.N.Y.: 13,187 Cases, 96.4% Dismissal Rate

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

13,187

Ch.13 Cases Filed

96.4%

Dismissal Rate

53.5%

Prior Filer Rate

58.2%

Pro Se Rate

The E.D.N.Y. in New York processed 13,187 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years
2020 and 2024, according to data from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database.
Of cases with known outcomes, 96.4% were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --
ranking this district #1 out of 91 measured districts nationally.

The national Chapter 13 dismissal rate for the same period is 75.6%, making E.D.N.Y.'s
rate far above the national average.

Key Statistics

MetricE.D.N.Y.National

Ch.13 Cases Filed (FY2020-2024)13,187834,483

Dismissal Rate96.4%75.6%

Prior Filer Rate53.5%36.6%

Prior Filer Discharge Rate1.8%15.1%

Pro Se Rate58.2%6.7%

Est. 1328(f) Violations5013,856

What This Means

A high dismissal rate means that most debtors who enter Chapter 13 in this district do not complete
their repayment plans. Dismissal can occur for many reasons -- missed payments, failure to file required
documents, inability to propose a confirmable plan, or voluntary dismissal by the debtor.

Dismissal rate: 96.4% -- More than three out of four Chapter 13 cases
filed in E.D.N.Y. end in dismissal rather than discharge. This is significantly above the national
average of 75.6%.

The prior filer rate of 53.5% indicates that a large share of Chapter 13 debtors in this
district have filed bankruptcy at least once before. Nationally, prior filers complete their Chapter 13
plans at a rate of just 15.1% -- roughly a third lower than the overall
discharge rate.

The pro se rate of 58.2% is notably high. Debtors without attorney representation face
significantly higher dismissal rates in Chapter 13, where the procedural requirements for plan
confirmation are complex. See Pro Se Bankruptcy Guide for more data.

Related Resources

Dismissal Rate Data -- interactive dismissal rate data for all 94 districts

Discharge Eligibility Screener -- check bankruptcy discharge eligibility under Section 1328(f)

Where Chapter 13 Fails: The 15 Worst Districts

Repeat Bankruptcy Filers by District

Methodology: Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (FJC IDB),
covering Chapter 13 cases filed in federal fiscal years 2020-2024. Dismissal and discharge rates are calculated
among cases with known dispositions. Prior filer rates reflect cases where a prior bankruptcy filing was identified
in the FJC record. Estimated 1328(f) violations are modeled based on discharge-despite-prior-filing patterns and
statutory waiting periods. This analysis was conducted by the Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team.

the Section 1328(f) waiting period between 2015 and 2024. No federal court currently screens for
these violations automatically. The Open Bankruptcy Project's free Discharge Eligibility Screener is the
only public tool available to check 1328(f) eligibility.

## Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in N.D. Tex.: 17,147 Cases, 86.4% Dismissal Rate -- Open Bankruptcy Project
Source: https://openbankruptcyproject.org/news/chapter-13-n-d-tex-bankruptcy-data.html

Open Bankruptcy Project

News
Research
About
Contact

KANSAS CITY, April 3, 2026 --

Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in N.D. Tex.: 17,147 Cases, 86.4% Dismissal Rate

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

17,147

Ch.13 Cases Filed

86.4%

Dismissal Rate

35.8%

Prior Filer Rate

6.2%

Pro Se Rate

The N.D. Tex. in Texas processed 17,147 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years
2020 and 2024, according to data from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database.
Of cases with known outcomes, 86.4% were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --
ranking this district #5 out of 91 measured districts nationally.

The national Chapter 13 dismissal rate for the same period is 75.6%, making N.D. Tex.'s
rate above the national average.

MetricN.D. Tex.National

Ch.13 Cases Filed (FY2020-2024)17,147834,483

Pro Se Rate6.2%6.7%

A high dismissal rate means that most debtors who enter Chapter 13 in this district do not complete
their repayment plans. Dismissal can occur for many reasons -- missed payments, failure to file required

Dismissal rate: 86.4% -- More than three out of four Chapter 13 cases
filed in N.D. Tex. end in dismissal rather than discharge. This is significantly above the national

Methodology: Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (FJC IDB),
covering Chapter 13 cases filed in federal fiscal years 2020-2024. Dismissal and discharge rates are calculated

## Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in N.D. Ga.: 40,911 Cases, 85.8% Dismissal Rate -- Open Bankruptcy Project
Source: https://openbankruptcyproject.org/news/chapter-13-n-d-ga-bankruptcy-data.html

Open Bankruptcy Project

News
Research
About
Contact

KANSAS CITY, April 3, 2026 --

Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in N.D. Ga.: 40,911 Cases, 85.8% Dismissal Rate

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

40,911

Ch.13 Cases Filed

85.8%

Dismissal Rate

48.2%

Prior Filer Rate

8.0%

Pro Se Rate

The N.D. Ga. in Georgia processed 40,911 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years
2020 and 2024, according to data from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database.
Of cases with known outcomes, 85.8% were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --
ranking this district #6 out of 91 measured districts nationally.

The national Chapter 13 dismissal rate for the same period is 75.6%, making N.D. Ga.'s
rate above the national average.

Key Statistics

MetricN.D. Ga.National

Ch.13 Cases Filed (FY2020-2024)40,911834,483

Dismissal Rate85.8%75.6%

Prior Filer Rate48.2%36.6%

Prior Filer Discharge Rate9.2%15.1%

Pro Se Rate8.0%6.7%

Est. 1328(f) Violations64813,856

What This Means

A high dismissal rate means that most debtors who enter Chapter 13 in this district do not complete
their repayment plans. Dismissal can occur for many reasons -- missed payments, failure to file required
documents, inability to propose a confirmable plan, or voluntary dismissal by the debtor.

Dismissal rate: 85.8% -- More than three out of four Chapter 13 cases
filed in N.D. Ga. end in dismissal rather than discharge. This is significantly above the national
average of 75.6%.

The prior filer rate of 48.2% indicates that a large share of Chapter 13 debtors in this
district have filed bankruptcy at least once before. Nationally, prior filers complete their Chapter 13
plans at a rate of just 15.1% -- roughly a third lower than the overall
discharge rate.

An estimated 648 discharges in this district may have been granted in violation of
the Section 1328(f) waiting period between 2015 and 2024. No federal court currently screens for
these violations automatically. The Open Bankruptcy Project's free Discharge Eligibility Screener is the
only public tool available to check 1328(f) eligibility.

## Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in D.D.C.: 397 Cases, 85.7% Dismissal Rate -- Open Bankruptcy Project
Source: https://openbankruptcyproject.org/news/chapter-13-d-d-c-bankruptcy-data.html

Open Bankruptcy Project

News
Research
About
Contact

KANSAS CITY, April 3, 2026 --

Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in D.D.C.: 397 Cases, 85.7% Dismissal Rate

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

397

Ch.13 Cases Filed

85.7%

Dismissal Rate

43.1%

Prior Filer Rate

30.0%

Pro Se Rate

The D.D.C. in District of Columbia processed 397 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years
2020 and 2024, according to data from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database.
Of cases with known outcomes, 85.7% were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --
ranking this district #7 out of 91 measured districts nationally.

The national Chapter 13 dismissal rate for the same period is 75.6%, making D.D.C.'s
rate above the national average.

Key Statistics

MetricD.D.C.National

Ch.13 Cases Filed (FY2020-2024)397834,483

Dismissal Rate85.7%75.6%

Prior Filer Rate43.1%36.6%

Prior Filer Discharge Rate4.9%15.1%

Pro Se Rate30.0%6.7%

Est. 1328(f) Violations313,856

What This Means

A high dismissal rate means that most debtors who enter Chapter 13 in this district do not complete
their repayment plans. Dismissal can occur for many reasons -- missed payments, failure to file required
documents, inability to propose a confirmable plan, or voluntary dismissal by the debtor.

Dismissal rate: 85.7% -- More than three out of four Chapter 13 cases
filed in D.D.C. end in dismissal rather than discharge. This is significantly above the national
average of 75.6%.

The prior filer rate of 43.1% indicates that a large share of Chapter 13 debtors in this
district have filed bankruptcy at least once before. Nationally, prior filers complete their Chapter 13
plans at a rate of just 15.1% -- roughly a third lower than the overall
discharge rate.

The pro se rate of 30.0% is notably high. Debtors without attorney representation face
significantly higher dismissal rates in Chapter 13, where the procedural requirements for plan
confirmation are complex. See Pro Se Bankruptcy Guide for more data.

Related Resources

Dismissal Rate Data -- interactive dismissal rate data for all 94 districts

Discharge Eligibility Screener -- check bankruptcy discharge eligibility under Section 1328(f)

in the FJC record. Estimated 1328(f) violations are modeled based on discharge-despite-prior-filing patterns and
statutory waiting periods. This analysis was conducted by the Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team.

A high dismissal rate means that most debtors who enter Chapter 13 in this district do not complete
their repayment plans. Dismissal can occur for many reasons -- missed payments, failure to file required
documents, inability to propose a confirmable plan, or voluntary dismissal by the debtor.

Dismissal rate: 81.5% -- More than three out of four Chapter 13 cases
filed in D. Maryland end in dismissal rather than discharge. This is significantly above the national
average of 75.6%.

The prior filer rate of 41.9% indicates that a large share of Chapter 13 debtors in this
district have filed bankruptcy at least once before. Nationally, prior filers complete their Chapter 13
plans at a rate of just 15.1% -- roughly a third lower than the overall
discharge rate.

An estimated 201 discharges in this district may have been granted in violation of
the Section 1328(f) waiting period between 2015 and 2024. No federal court currently screens for
these violations automatically. The Open Bankruptcy Project's free Discharge Eligibility Screener is the
only public tool available to check 1328(f) eligibility.

Related Resources

Dismissal Rate Data -- interactive dismissal rate data for all 94 districts

Discharge Eligibility Screener -- check bankruptcy discharge eligibility under Section 1328(f)

Where Chapter 13 Fails: The 15 Worst Districts

Repeat Bankruptcy Filers by District

Methodology: Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (FJC IDB),
covering Chapter 13 cases filed in federal fiscal years 2020-2024. Dismissal and discharge rates are calculated
among cases with known dispositions. Prior filer rates reflect cases where a prior bankruptcy filing was identified
in the FJC record. Estimated 1328(f) violations are modeled based on discharge-despite-prior-filing patterns and
statutory waiting periods. This analysis was conducted by the Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team.

## Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in S.D.N.Y.: 4,263 Cases, 80.0% Dismissal Rate -- Open Bankruptcy Project
Source: https://openbankruptcyproject.org/news/chapter-13-s-d-n-y-bankruptcy-data.html

Open Bankruptcy Project

News
Research
About
Contact

KANSAS CITY, April 3, 2026 --

Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in S.D.N.Y.: 4,263 Cases, 80.0% Dismissal Rate

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

4,263

Ch.13 Cases Filed

80.0%

Dismissal Rate

36.8%

Prior Filer Rate

20.8%

Pro Se Rate

The S.D.N.Y. in New York processed 4,263 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years
2020 and 2024, according to data from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database.
Of cases with known outcomes, 80.0% were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --
ranking this district #14 out of 91 measured districts nationally.

The national Chapter 13 dismissal rate for the same period is 75.6%, making S.D.N.Y.'s
rate near the national average.

Key Statistics

MetricS.D.N.Y.National

Ch.13 Cases Filed (FY2020-2024)4,263834,483

Dismissal Rate80.0%75.6%

Prior Filer Rate36.8%36.6%

Prior Filer Discharge Rate9.2%15.1%

Pro Se Rate20.8%6.7%

Est. 1328(f) Violations5113,856

What This Means

A high dismissal rate means that most debtors who enter Chapter 13 in this district do not complete
their repayment plans. Dismissal can occur for many reasons -- missed payments, failure to file required
documents, inability to propose a confirmable plan, or voluntary dismissal by the debtor.

Dismissal rate: 80.0% -- More than three out of four Chapter 13 cases
filed in S.D.N.Y. end in dismissal rather than discharge. This is significantly above the national
average of 75.6%.

The pro se rate of 20.8% is notably high. Debtors without attorney representation face
significantly higher dismissal rates in Chapter 13, where the procedural requirements for plan
confirmation are complex. See Pro Se Bankruptcy Guide for more data.

An estimated 51 discharges in this district may have been granted in violation of
the Section 1328(f) waiting period between 2015 and 2024. No federal court currently screens for
these violations automatically. The Open Bankruptcy Project's free Discharge Eligibility Screener is the
only public tool available to check 1328(f) eligibility.

Where Chapter 13 Fails: The 15 Worst Districts

Repeat Bankruptcy Filers by District

Methodology: Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (FJC IDB),
covering Chapter 13 cases filed in federal fiscal years 2020-2024. Dismissal and discharge rates are calculated
among cases with known dispositions. Prior filer rates reflect cases where a prior bankruptcy filing was identified
in the FJC record. Estimated 1328(f) violations are modeled based on discharge-despite-prior-filing patterns and
statutory waiting periods. This analysis was conducted by the Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team.

Open Bankruptcy Project

News
Research
About
Contact

KANSAS CITY, April 3, 2026 --

Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in E.D. Pa.: 10,211 Cases, 77.7% Dismissal Rate

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

10,211

Ch.13 Cases Filed

77.7%

Dismissal Rate

35.9%

Prior Filer Rate

6.8%

Pro Se Rate

The E.D. Pa. in Pennsylvania processed 10,211 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years
2020 and 2024, according to data from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database.
Of cases with known outcomes, 77.7% were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --
ranking this district #22 out of 91 measured districts nationally.

The national Chapter 13 dismissal rate for the same period is 75.6%, making E.D. Pa.'s
rate near the national average.

Key Statistics

MetricE.D. Pa.National

Ch.13 Cases Filed (FY2020-2024)10,211834,483

Dismissal Rate77.7%75.6%

Prior Filer Rate35.9%36.6%

Prior Filer Discharge Rate12.3%15.1%

Pro Se Rate6.8%6.7%

Est. 1328(f) Violations15013,856

What This Means

A high dismissal rate means that most debtors who enter Chapter 13 in this district do not complete
their repayment plans. Dismissal can occur for many reasons -- missed payments, failure to file required
documents, inability to propose a confirmable plan, or voluntary dismissal by the debtor.

Dismissal rate: 77.7% -- More than three out of four Chapter 13 cases
filed in E.D. Pa. end in dismissal rather than discharge. This is significantly above the national
average of 75.6%.

An estimated 150 discharges in this district may have been granted in violation of
the Section 1328(f) waiting period between 2015 and 2024. No federal court currently screens for
these violations automatically. The Open Bankruptcy Project's free Discharge Eligibility Screener is the
only public tool available to check 1328(f) eligibility.

Methodology: Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (FJC IDB),
covering Chapter 13 cases filed in federal fiscal years 2020-2024. Dismissal and discharge rates are calculated
among cases with known dispositions. Prior filer rates reflect cases where a prior bankruptcy filing was identified
in the FJC record. Estimated 1328(f) violations are modeled based on discharge-despite-prior-filing patterns and
statutory waiting periods. This analysis was conducted by the Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team.

Source: https://openbankruptcyproject.org/news/chapter-13-s-d-cal-bankruptcy-data.html

Open Bankruptcy Project

News
Research
About
Contact

KANSAS CITY, April 3, 2026 --

Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in S.D. Cal.: 2,932 Cases, 77.2% Dismissal Rate

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

2,932

Ch.13 Cases Filed

77.2%

Dismissal Rate

38.9%

Prior Filer Rate

17.7%

Pro Se Rate

The S.D. Cal. in California processed 2,932 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years
2020 and 2024, according to data from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database.
Of cases with known outcomes, 77.2% were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --
ranking this district #24 out of 91 measured districts nationally.

The national Chapter 13 dismissal rate for the same period is 75.6%, making S.D. Cal.'s
rate near the national average.

Key Statistics

MetricS.D. Cal.National

Ch.13 Cases Filed (FY2020-2024)2,932834,483

Dismissal Rate77.2%75.6%

Prior Filer Rate38.9%36.6%

Prior Filer Discharge Rate13.0%15.1%

Pro Se Rate17.7%6.7%

Est. 1328(f) Violations4713,856

What This Means

A high dismissal rate means that most debtors who enter Chapter 13 in this district do not complete
their repayment plans. Dismissal can occur for many reasons -- missed payments, failure to file required
documents, inability to propose a confirmable plan, or voluntary dismissal by the debtor.

Dismissal rate: 77.2% -- More than three out of four Chapter 13 cases
filed in S.D. Cal. end in dismissal rather than discharge. This is significantly above the national
average of 75.6%.

The pro se rate of 17.7% is notably high. Debtors without attorney representation face
significantly higher dismissal rates in Chapter 13, where the procedural requirements for plan
confirmation are complex. See Pro Se Bankruptcy Guide for more data.

Ch.13 Cases Filed

2.9%

MetricE.D. Mo.National

Ch.13 Cases Filed (FY2020-2024)11,116834,483

Dismissal Rate76.1%75.6%

Prior Filer Rate44.4%36.6%

Prior Filer Discharge Rate17.5%15.1%

Pro Se Rate2.9%6.7%

Est. 1328(f) Violations25813,856

What This Means

A high dismissal rate means that most debtors who enter Chapter 13 in this district do not complete
their repayment plans. Dismissal can occur for many reasons -- missed payments, failure to file required
documents, inability to propose a confirmable plan, or voluntary dismissal by the debtor.

Dismissal rate: 76.1% -- More than three out of four Chapter 13 cases
filed in E.D. Mo. end in dismissal rather than discharge. This is significantly above the national
average of 75.6%.

The prior filer rate of 44.4% indicates that a large share of Chapter 13 debtors in this
district have filed bankruptcy at least once before. Nationally, prior filers complete their Chapter 13
plans at a rate of just 15.1% -- roughly a third lower than the overall
discharge rate.

An estimated 258 discharges in this district may have been granted in violation of
the Section 1328(f) waiting period between 2015 and 2024. No federal court currently screens for
these violations automatically. The Open Bankruptcy Project's free Discharge Eligibility Screener is the
only public tool available to check 1328(f) eligibility.

Related Resources

Dismissal Rate Data -- interactive dismissal rate data for all 94 districts

Discharge Eligibility Screener -- check bankruptcy discharge eligibility under Section 1328(f)

Where Chapter 13 Fails: The 15 Worst Districts

Repeat Bankruptcy Filers by District

Methodology: Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (FJC IDB),
covering Chapter 13 cases filed in federal fiscal years 2020-2024. Dismissal and discharge rates are calculated
among cases with known dispositions. Prior filer rates reflect cases where a prior bankruptcy filing was identified
in the FJC record. Estimated 1328(f) violations are modeled based on discharge-despite-prior-filing patterns and
statutory waiting periods. This analysis was conducted by the Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team.

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

5,309

Ch.13 Cases Filed

76.1%

Dismissal Rate

39.2%

Prior Filer Rate

9.7%

Pro Se Rate

The D. Nev. in Nevada processed 5,309 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years
2020 and 2024, according to data from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database.
Of cases with known outcomes, 76.1% were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --
ranking this district #31 out of 91 measured districts nationally.

The national Chapter 13 dismissal rate for the same period is 75.6%, making D. Nev.'s
rate near the national average.

Dismissal rate: 76.1% -- More than three out of four Chapter 13 cases

## Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in D. New Jersey: 21,981 Cases, 74.7% Dismissal Rate -- Open Bankruptcy Project
Source: https://openbankruptcyproject.org/news/chapter-13-d-new-jersey-bankruptcy-data.html

Open Bankruptcy Project

News
Research
About
Contact

KANSAS CITY, April 3, 2026 --

Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in D. New Jersey: 21,981 Cases, 74.7% Dismissal Rate

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

21,981

Ch.13 Cases Filed

74.7%

Dismissal Rate

38.0%

Prior Filer Rate

7.4%

Pro Se Rate

The D. New Jersey in New Jersey processed 21,981 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years
2020 and 2024, according to data from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database.
Of cases with known outcomes, 74.7% were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --
ranking this district #39 out of 91 measured districts nationally.

The national Chapter 13 dismissal rate for the same period is 75.6%, making D. New Jersey's
rate near the national average.

Key Statistics

MetricD. New JerseyNational

Ch.13 Cases Filed (FY2020-2024)21,981834,483

Dismissal Rate74.7%75.6%

Prior Filer Rate38.0%36.6%

Prior Filer Discharge Rate16.5%15.1%

Pro Se Rate7.4%6.7%

Est. 1328(f) Violations44213,856

What This Means

A high dismissal rate means that most debtors who enter Chapter 13 in this district do not complete
their repayment plans. Dismissal can occur for many reasons -- missed payments, failure to file required
documents, inability to propose a confirmable plan, or voluntary dismissal by the debtor.

An estimated 442 discharges in this district may have been granted in violation of
the Section 1328(f) waiting period between 2015 and 2024. No federal court currently screens for
these violations automatically. The Open Bankruptcy Project's free Discharge Eligibility Screener is the
only public tool available to check 1328(f) eligibility.

Related Resources

Dismissal Rate Data -- interactive dismissal rate data for all 94 districts

Discharge Eligibility Screener -- check bankruptcy discharge eligibility under Section 1328(f)

Where Chapter 13 Fails: The 15 Worst Districts

Repeat Bankruptcy Filers by District

Methodology: Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (FJC IDB),
covering Chapter 13 cases filed in federal fiscal years 2020-2024. Dismissal and discharge rates are calculated
among cases with known dispositions. Prior filer rates reflect cases where a prior bankruptcy filing was identified
in the FJC record. Estimated 1328(f) violations are modeled based on discharge-despite-prior-filing patterns and
statutory waiting periods. This analysis was conducted by the Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team.

Free, open-source bankruptcy transparency. No ads. No affiliate links. Supported by donations.

## Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in D. Ariz.: 8,695 Cases, 74.5% Dismissal Rate -- Open Bankruptcy Project
Source: https://openbankruptcyproject.org/news/chapter-13-d-ariz-bankruptcy-data.html

Open Bankruptcy Project

News
Research
About
Contact

KANSAS CITY, April 3, 2026 --

Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in D. Ariz.: 8,695 Cases, 74.5% Dismissal Rate

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

8,695

Ch.13 Cases Filed

74.5%

Dismissal Rate

34.7%

Prior Filer Rate

13.5%

Pro Se Rate

The D. Ariz. in Arizona processed 8,695 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years
2020 and 2024, according to data from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database.
Of cases with known outcomes, 74.5% were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --
ranking this district #40 out of 91 measured districts nationally.

The national Chapter 13 dismissal rate for the same period is 75.6%, making D. Ariz.'s
rate near the national average.

Key Statistics

MetricD. Ariz.National

Ch.13 Cases Filed (FY2020-2024)8,695834,483

Dismissal Rate74.5%75.6%

Prior Filer Rate34.7%36.6%

Prior Filer Discharge Rate15.9%15.1%

Pro Se Rate13.5%6.7%

Est. 1328(f) Violations14513,856

What This Means

A high dismissal rate means that most debtors who enter Chapter 13 in this district do not complete
their repayment plans. Dismissal can occur for many reasons -- missed payments, failure to file required
documents, inability to propose a confirmable plan, or voluntary dismissal by the debtor.

An estimated 145 discharges in this district may have been granted in violation of
the Section 1328(f) waiting period between 2015 and 2024. No federal court currently screens for
these violations automatically. The Open Bankruptcy Project's free Discharge Eligibility Screener is the
only public tool available to check 1328(f) eligibility.

Related Resources

Discharge Eligibility Screener -- check bankruptcy discharge eligibility under Section 1328(f)

Where Chapter 13 Fails: The 15 Worst Districts

Repeat Bankruptcy Filers by District

Methodology: Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (FJC IDB),
covering Chapter 13 cases filed in federal fiscal years 2020-2024. Dismissal and discharge rates are calculated
among cases with known dispositions. Prior filer rates reflect cases where a prior bankruptcy filing was identified
in the FJC record. Estimated 1328(f) violations are modeled based on discharge-despite-prior-filing patterns and
statutory waiting periods. This analysis was conducted by the Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team.

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

10,209

Ch.13 Cases Filed

73.9%

Dismissal Rate

34.9%

Prior Filer Rate

3.7%

Pro Se Rate

The N.D. Ind. in Indiana processed 10,209 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years
2020 and 2024, according to data from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database.
Of cases with known outcomes, 73.9% were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --
ranking this district #41 out of 91 measured districts nationally.

The national Chapter 13 dismissal rate for the same period is 75.6%, making N.D. Ind.'s
rate near the national average.

MetricN.D. Ind.National

Prior Filer Discharge Rate17.7%15.1%

Pro Se Rate3.7%6.7%

Est. 1328(f) Violations17413,856

A high dismissal rate means that most debtors who enter Chapter 13 in this district do not complete
their repayment plans. Dismissal can occur for many reasons -- missed payments, failure to file required
documents, inability to propose a confirmable plan, or voluntary dismissal by the debtor.

An estimated 174 discharges in this district may have been granted in violation of
the Section 1328(f) waiting period between 2015 and 2024. No federal court currently screens for

## Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in N.D. Ohio: 11,378 Cases, 72.3% Dismissal Rate -- Open Bankruptcy Project
Source: https://openbankruptcyproject.org/news/chapter-13-n-d-ohio-bankruptcy-data.html

Open Bankruptcy Project

News
Research
About
Contact

KANSAS CITY, April 3, 2026 --

Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in N.D. Ohio: 11,378 Cases, 72.3% Dismissal Rate

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

11,378

Ch.13 Cases Filed

72.3%

Dismissal Rate

41.4%

Prior Filer Rate

4.6%

Pro Se Rate

The N.D. Ohio in Ohio processed 11,378 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years
2020 and 2024, according to data from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database.
Of cases with known outcomes, 72.3% were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --
ranking this district #44 out of 91 measured districts nationally.

The national Chapter 13 dismissal rate for the same period is 75.6%, making N.D. Ohio's
rate near the national average.

Key Statistics

MetricN.D. OhioNational

Ch.13 Cases Filed (FY2020-2024)11,378834,483

Dismissal Rate72.3%75.6%

Prior Filer Rate41.4%36.6%

Prior Filer Discharge Rate17.7%15.1%

Pro Se Rate4.6%6.7%

Est. 1328(f) Violations24113,856

What This Means

A high dismissal rate means that most debtors who enter Chapter 13 in this district do not complete
their repayment plans. Dismissal can occur for many reasons -- missed payments, failure to file required
documents, inability to propose a confirmable plan, or voluntary dismissal by the debtor.

The prior filer rate of 41.4% indicates that a large share of Chapter 13 debtors in this
district have filed bankruptcy at least once before. Nationally, prior filers complete their Chapter 13
plans at a rate of just 15.1% -- roughly a third lower than the overall
discharge rate.

An estimated 241 discharges in this district may have been granted in violation of
the Section 1328(f) waiting period between 2015 and 2024. No federal court currently screens for
these violations automatically. The Open Bankruptcy Project's free Discharge Eligibility Screener is the
only public tool available to check 1328(f) eligibility.

Related Resources

Dismissal Rate Data -- interactive dismissal rate data for all 94 districts

Discharge Eligibility Screener -- check bankruptcy discharge eligibility under Section 1328(f)

Where Chapter 13 Fails: The 15 Worst Districts

Repeat Bankruptcy Filers by District

Methodology: Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (FJC IDB),
covering Chapter 13 cases filed in federal fiscal years 2020-2024. Dismissal and discharge rates are calculated
among cases with known dispositions. Prior filer rates reflect cases where a prior bankruptcy filing was identified
in the FJC record. Estimated 1328(f) violations are modeled based on discharge-despite-prior-filing patterns and
statutory waiting periods. This analysis was conducted by the Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team.

## Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in W.D. Okla.: 3,637 Cases, 71.6% Dismissal Rate -- Open Bankruptcy Project

Open Bankruptcy Project

News
Research
About
Contact

KANSAS CITY, April 3, 2026 --

Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in W.D. Okla.: 3,637 Cases, 71.6% Dismissal Rate

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

3,637

Ch.13 Cases Filed

71.6%

Dismissal Rate

40.1%

Prior Filer Rate

5.2%

Pro Se Rate

The W.D. Okla. in Oklahoma processed 3,637 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years
2020 and 2024, according to data from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database.
Of cases with known outcomes, 71.6% were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --
ranking this district #45 out of 91 measured districts nationally.

The national Chapter 13 dismissal rate for the same period is 75.6%, making W.D. Okla.'s
rate near the national average.

Key Statistics

MetricW.D. Okla.National

Ch.13 Cases Filed (FY2020-2024)3,637834,483

Dismissal Rate71.6%75.6%

Prior Filer Rate40.1%36.6%

Prior Filer Discharge Rate23.5%15.1%

Pro Se Rate5.2%6.7%

Est. 1328(f) Violations8413,856

What This Means

A high dismissal rate means that most debtors who enter Chapter 13 in this district do not complete
their repayment plans. Dismissal can occur for many reasons -- missed payments, failure to file required
documents, inability to propose a confirmable plan, or voluntary dismissal by the debtor.

The prior filer rate of 40.1% indicates that a large share of Chapter 13 debtors in this
district have filed bankruptcy at least once before. Nationally, prior filers complete their Chapter 13
plans at a rate of just 15.1% -- roughly a third lower than the overall
discharge rate.

An estimated 84 discharges in this district may have been granted in violation of
the Section 1328(f) waiting period between 2015 and 2024. No federal court currently screens for
these violations automatically. The Open Bankruptcy Project's free Discharge Eligibility Screener is the
only public tool available to check 1328(f) eligibility.

## Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in N.D. Iowa: 593 Cases, 71.4% Dismissal Rate -- Open Bankruptcy Project
Source: https://openbankruptcyproject.org/news/chapter-13-n-d-iowa-bankruptcy-data.html

Open Bankruptcy Project

News
Research
About
Contact

KANSAS CITY, April 3, 2026 --

Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in N.D. Iowa: 593 Cases, 71.4% Dismissal Rate

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

593

Ch.13 Cases Filed

71.4%

Dismissal Rate

33.2%

Prior Filer Rate

18.4%

Pro Se Rate

The N.D. Iowa in Iowa processed 593 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years
2020 and 2024, according to data from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database.
Of cases with known outcomes, 71.4% were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --
ranking this district #46 out of 91 measured districts nationally.

The national Chapter 13 dismissal rate for the same period is 75.6%, making N.D. Iowa's
rate near the national average.

Key Statistics

MetricN.D. IowaNational

Ch.13 Cases Filed (FY2020-2024)593834,483

Dismissal Rate71.4%75.6%

Prior Filer Rate33.2%36.6%

Prior Filer Discharge Rate20.1%15.1%

Pro Se Rate18.4%6.7%

Est. 1328(f) Violations1213,856

What This Means

A high dismissal rate means that most debtors who enter Chapter 13 in this district do not complete
their repayment plans. Dismissal can occur for many reasons -- missed payments, failure to file required
documents, inability to propose a confirmable plan, or voluntary dismissal by the debtor.

The pro se rate of 18.4% is notably high. Debtors without attorney representation face
significantly higher dismissal rates in Chapter 13, where the procedural requirements for plan
confirmation are complex. See Pro Se Bankruptcy Guide for more data.

Related Resources

Dismissal Rate Data -- interactive dismissal rate data for all 94 districts

Discharge Eligibility Screener -- check bankruptcy discharge eligibility under Section 1328(f)

Where Chapter 13 Fails: The 15 Worst Districts

Repeat Bankruptcy Filers by District

Methodology: Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (FJC IDB),
covering Chapter 13 cases filed in federal fiscal years 2020-2024. Dismissal and discharge rates are calculated
among cases with known dispositions. Prior filer rates reflect cases where a prior bankruptcy filing was identified
in the FJC record. Estimated 1328(f) violations are modeled based on discharge-despite-prior-filing patterns and
statutory waiting periods. This analysis was conducted by the Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team.

Published April 3, 2026

26,557

Ch.13 Cases Filed

71.3%

Dismissal Rate

23.2%

Prior Filer Rate

7.0%

The S.D. Fla. in Florida processed 26,557 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years

Of cases with known outcomes, 71.3% were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --

Where Chapter 13 Fails: The 15 Worst Districts

Repeat Bankruptcy Filers by District

Methodology: Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (FJC IDB),
covering Chapter 13 cases filed in federal fiscal years 2020-2024. Dismissal and discharge rates are calculated
among cases with known dispositions. Prior filer rates reflect cases where a prior bankruptcy filing was identified
in the FJC record. Estimated 1328(f) violations are modeled based on discharge-despite-prior-filing patterns and
statutory waiting periods. This analysis was conducted by the Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team.

News
Research
About
Contact

KANSAS CITY, April 3, 2026 --

Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in E.D. La.: 6,571 Cases, 71.2% Dismissal Rate

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

6,571

Ch.13 Cases Filed

71.2%

Dismissal Rate

23.3%

Prior Filer Rate

6.3%

Pro Se Rate

The E.D. La. in Louisiana processed 6,571 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years
2020 and 2024, according to data from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database.
Of cases with known outcomes, 71.2% were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --
ranking this district #48 out of 91 measured districts nationally.

The national Chapter 13 dismissal rate for the same period is 75.6%, making E.D. La.'s
rate near the national average.

Key Statistics

MetricE.D. La.National

Ch.13 Cases Filed (FY2020-2024)6,571834,483

Dismissal Rate71.2%75.6%

Prior Filer Rate23.3%36.6%

Prior Filer Discharge Rate18.5%15.1%

Pro Se Rate6.3%6.7%

Est. 1328(f) Violations8413,856

What This Means

A high dismissal rate means that most debtors who enter Chapter 13 in this district do not complete
their repayment plans. Dismissal can occur for many reasons -- missed payments, failure to file required
documents, inability to propose a confirmable plan, or voluntary dismissal by the debtor.

An estimated 84 discharges in this district may have been granted in violation of
the Section 1328(f) waiting period between 2015 and 2024. No federal court currently screens for
these violations automatically. The Open Bankruptcy Project's free Discharge Eligibility Screener is the
only public tool available to check 1328(f) eligibility.

Methodology: Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (FJC IDB),
covering Chapter 13 cases filed in federal fiscal years 2020-2024. Dismissal and discharge rates are calculated
among cases with known dispositions. Prior filer rates reflect cases where a prior bankruptcy filing was identified
in the FJC record. Estimated 1328(f) violations are modeled based on discharge-despite-prior-filing patterns and
statutory waiting periods. This analysis was conducted by the Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team.

## Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in N.D. Cal.: 8,358 Cases, 70.9% Dismissal Rate -- Open Bankruptcy Project
Source: https://openbankruptcyproject.org/news/chapter-13-n-d-cal-bankruptcy-data.html

Open Bankruptcy Project

News
Research
About
Contact

KANSAS CITY, April 3, 2026 --

Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in N.D. Cal.: 8,358 Cases, 70.9% Dismissal Rate

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

8,358

Ch.13 Cases Filed

70.9%

Dismissal Rate

31.7%

Prior Filer Rate

17.6%

Pro Se Rate

The N.D. Cal. in California processed 8,358 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years
2020 and 2024, according to data from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database.
Of cases with known outcomes, 70.9% were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --
ranking this district #50 out of 91 measured districts nationally.

The national Chapter 13 dismissal rate for the same period is 75.6%, making N.D. Cal.'s
rate near the national average.

Key Statistics

MetricN.D. Cal.National

Ch.13 Cases Filed (FY2020-2024)8,358834,483

Dismissal Rate70.9%75.6%

Prior Filer Rate31.7%36.6%

Prior Filer Discharge Rate13.4%15.1%

Pro Se Rate17.6%6.7%

Est. 1328(f) Violations11413,856

What This Means

A high dismissal rate means that most debtors who enter Chapter 13 in this district do not complete
their repayment plans. Dismissal can occur for many reasons -- missed payments, failure to file required
documents, inability to propose a confirmable plan, or voluntary dismissal by the debtor.

The pro se rate of 17.6% is notably high. Debtors without attorney representation face
significantly higher dismissal rates in Chapter 13, where the procedural requirements for plan
confirmation are complex. See Pro Se Bankruptcy Guide for more data.

An estimated 114 discharges in this district may have been granted in violation of
the Section 1328(f) waiting period between 2015 and 2024. No federal court currently screens for
these violations automatically. The Open Bankruptcy Project's free Discharge Eligibility Screener is the
only public tool available to check 1328(f) eligibility.

Related Resources

Dismissal Rate Data -- interactive dismissal rate data for all 94 districts

Discharge Eligibility Screener -- check bankruptcy discharge eligibility under Section 1328(f)

Repeat Bankruptcy Filers by District

Methodology: Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (FJC IDB),
covering Chapter 13 cases filed in federal fiscal years 2020-2024. Dismissal and discharge rates are calculated
among cases with known dispositions. Prior filer rates reflect cases where a prior bankruptcy filing was identified
in the FJC record. Estimated 1328(f) violations are modeled based on discharge-despite-prior-filing patterns and
statutory waiting periods. This analysis was conducted by the Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team.

Open Bankruptcy Project

News
Research
About
Contact

KANSAS CITY, April 3, 2026 --

Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in M.D. Tenn.: 11,524 Cases, 70.7% Dismissal Rate

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

11,524

Ch.13 Cases Filed

70.7%

Dismissal Rate

49.0%

Prior Filer Rate

2.5%

Pro Se Rate

The M.D. Tenn. in Tennessee processed 11,524 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years
2020 and 2024, according to data from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database.
Of cases with known outcomes, 70.7% were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --
ranking this district #53 out of 91 measured districts nationally.

The national Chapter 13 dismissal rate for the same period is 75.6%, making M.D. Tenn.'s
rate near the national average.

Key Statistics

MetricM.D. Tenn.National

Ch.13 Cases Filed (FY2020-2024)11,524834,483

Dismissal Rate70.7%75.6%

Prior Filer Rate49.0%36.6%

Prior Filer Discharge Rate23.3%15.1%

Pro Se Rate2.5%6.7%

Est. 1328(f) Violations35413,856

What This Means

A high dismissal rate means that most debtors who enter Chapter 13 in this district do not complete
their repayment plans. Dismissal can occur for many reasons -- missed payments, failure to file required
documents, inability to propose a confirmable plan, or voluntary dismissal by the debtor.

The prior filer rate of 49.0% indicates that a large share of Chapter 13 debtors in this
district have filed bankruptcy at least once before. Nationally, prior filers complete their Chapter 13
plans at a rate of just 15.1% -- roughly a third lower than the overall
discharge rate.

An estimated 354 discharges in this district may have been granted in violation of
the Section 1328(f) waiting period between 2015 and 2024. No federal court currently screens for
these violations automatically. The Open Bankruptcy Project's free Discharge Eligibility Screener is the
only public tool available to check 1328(f) eligibility.

Repeat Bankruptcy Filers by District

Methodology: Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (FJC IDB),
covering Chapter 13 cases filed in federal fiscal years 2020-2024. Dismissal and discharge rates are calculated
among cases with known dispositions. Prior filer rates reflect cases where a prior bankruptcy filing was identified
in the FJC record. Estimated 1328(f) violations are modeled based on discharge-despite-prior-filing patterns and
statutory waiting periods. This analysis was conducted by the Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team.

Free, open-source bankruptcy transparency. No ads. No affiliate links. Supported by donations.

Published April 3, 2026

Ch.13 Cases Filed

69.4%

Dismissal Rate

24.8%

Prior Filer Rate

3.0%

Pro Se Rate

The E.D.N.C. in North Carolina processed 12,727 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years
2020 and 2024, according to data from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database.
Of cases with known outcomes, 69.4% were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --
ranking this district #57 out of 91 measured districts nationally.

The national Chapter 13 dismissal rate for the same period is 75.6%, making E.D.N.C.'s
rate below the national average.

Pro Se Rate3.0%6.7%

Est. 1328(f) Violations10213,856

What This Means

A high dismissal rate means that most debtors who enter Chapter 13 in this district do not complete
their repayment plans. Dismissal can occur for many reasons -- missed payments, failure to file required
documents, inability to propose a confirmable plan, or voluntary dismissal by the debtor.

An estimated 102 discharges in this district may have been granted in violation of
the Section 1328(f) waiting period between 2015 and 2024. No federal court currently screens for
these violations automatically. The Open Bankruptcy Project's free Discharge Eligibility Screener is the
only public tool available to check 1328(f) eligibility.

## Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in S.D. Iowa: 1,387 Cases, 69.2% Dismissal Rate -- Open Bankruptcy Project
Source: https://openbankruptcyproject.org/news/chapter-13-s-d-iowa-bankruptcy-data.html

Open Bankruptcy Project

News
Research
About
Contact

KANSAS CITY, April 3, 2026 --

Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in S.D. Iowa: 1,387 Cases, 69.2% Dismissal Rate

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

1,387

Ch.13 Cases Filed

69.2%

Dismissal Rate

33.8%

Prior Filer Rate

18.2%

Pro Se Rate

The S.D. Iowa in Iowa processed 1,387 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years
2020 and 2024, according to data from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database.
Of cases with known outcomes, 69.2% were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --
ranking this district #58 out of 91 measured districts nationally.

The national Chapter 13 dismissal rate for the same period is 75.6%, making S.D. Iowa's
rate below the national average.

Key Statistics

MetricS.D. IowaNational

Ch.13 Cases Filed (FY2020-2024)1,387834,483

Dismissal Rate69.2%75.6%

Prior Filer Rate33.8%36.6%

Prior Filer Discharge Rate22.2%15.1%

Pro Se Rate18.2%6.7%

Est. 1328(f) Violations2813,856

What This Means

A high dismissal rate means that most debtors who enter Chapter 13 in this district do not complete
their repayment plans. Dismissal can occur for many reasons -- missed payments, failure to file required
documents, inability to propose a confirmable plan, or voluntary dismissal by the debtor.

The pro se rate of 18.2% is notably high. Debtors without attorney representation face
significantly higher dismissal rates in Chapter 13, where the procedural requirements for plan
confirmation are complex. See Pro Se Bankruptcy Guide for more data.

## Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in D. Idaho: 1,082 Cases, 67.6% Dismissal Rate -- Open Bankruptcy Project
Source: https://openbankruptcyproject.org/news/chapter-13-d-idaho-bankruptcy-data.html

Open Bankruptcy Project

News
Research
About
Contact

KANSAS CITY, April 3, 2026 --

Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in D. Idaho: 1,082 Cases, 67.6% Dismissal Rate

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

1,082

Ch.13 Cases Filed

67.6%

Dismissal Rate

50.4%

Prior Filer Rate

9.3%

Pro Se Rate

The D. Idaho in Idaho processed 1,082 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years
2020 and 2024, according to data from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database.
Of cases with known outcomes, 67.6% were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --
ranking this district #62 out of 91 measured districts nationally.

The national Chapter 13 dismissal rate for the same period is 75.6%, making D. Idaho's
rate below the national average.

Key Statistics

MetricD. IdahoNational

Ch.13 Cases Filed (FY2020-2024)1,082834,483

Dismissal Rate67.6%75.6%

Prior Filer Rate50.4%36.6%

Prior Filer Discharge Rate26.7%15.1%

Pro Se Rate9.3%6.7%

Est. 1328(f) Violations4313,856

What This Means

A high dismissal rate means that most debtors who enter Chapter 13 in this district do not complete
their repayment plans. Dismissal can occur for many reasons -- missed payments, failure to file required
documents, inability to propose a confirmable plan, or voluntary dismissal by the debtor.

The prior filer rate of 50.4% indicates that a large share of Chapter 13 debtors in this
district have filed bankruptcy at least once before. Nationally, prior filers complete their Chapter 13
plans at a rate of just 15.1% -- roughly a third lower than the overall
discharge rate.

in the FJC record. Estimated 1328(f) violations are modeled based on discharge-despite-prior-filing patterns and
statutory waiting periods. This analysis was conducted by the Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team.

Open Bankruptcy Project

News
Research
About
Contact

KANSAS CITY, April 3, 2026 --

Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in S.D.W. Va.: 595 Cases, 66.8% Dismissal Rate

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

595

Ch.13 Cases Filed

66.8%

Dismissal Rate

25.5%

Prior Filer Rate

7.4%

Pro Se Rate

The S.D.W. Va. in West Virginia processed 595 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years
2020 and 2024, according to data from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database.
Of cases with known outcomes, 66.8% were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --
ranking this district #66 out of 91 measured districts nationally.

The national Chapter 13 dismissal rate for the same period is 75.6%, making S.D.W. Va.'s
rate below the national average.

Key Statistics

MetricS.D.W. Va.National

Ch.13 Cases Filed (FY2020-2024)595834,483

Dismissal Rate66.8%75.6%

Prior Filer Rate25.5%36.6%

Prior Filer Discharge Rate21.7%15.1%

Pro Se Rate7.4%6.7%

Est. 1328(f) Violations913,856

What This Means

A high dismissal rate means that most debtors who enter Chapter 13 in this district do not complete
their repayment plans. Dismissal can occur for many reasons -- missed payments, failure to file required
documents, inability to propose a confirmable plan, or voluntary dismissal by the debtor.

Discharge Eligibility Screener -- check bankruptcy discharge eligibility under Section 1328(f)

Where Chapter 13 Fails: The 15 Worst Districts

Repeat Bankruptcy Filers by District

Methodology: Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (FJC IDB),
covering Chapter 13 cases filed in federal fiscal years 2020-2024. Dismissal and discharge rates are calculated
among cases with known dispositions. Prior filer rates reflect cases where a prior bankruptcy filing was identified
in the FJC record. Estimated 1328(f) violations are modeled based on discharge-despite-prior-filing patterns and
statutory waiting periods. This analysis was conducted by the Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team.

## Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in C.D. Ill.: 3,037 Cases, 62.3% Dismissal Rate -- Open Bankruptcy Project
Source: https://openbankruptcyproject.org/news/chapter-13-c-d-ill-bankruptcy-data.html

Open Bankruptcy Project

News
Research
About
Contact

KANSAS CITY, April 3, 2026 --

Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in C.D. Ill.: 3,037 Cases, 62.3% Dismissal Rate

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

3,037

Ch.13 Cases Filed

62.3%

Dismissal Rate

28.4%

Prior Filer Rate

5.0%

Pro Se Rate

The C.D. Ill. in Illinois processed 3,037 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years
2020 and 2024, according to data from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database.
Of cases with known outcomes, 62.3% were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --
ranking this district #75 out of 91 measured districts nationally.

The national Chapter 13 dismissal rate for the same period is 75.6%, making C.D. Ill.'s
rate below the national average.

Key Statistics

MetricC.D. Ill.National

Ch.13 Cases Filed (FY2020-2024)3,037834,483

Dismissal Rate62.3%75.6%

Prior Filer Rate28.4%36.6%

Prior Filer Discharge Rate29.8%15.1%

Pro Se Rate5.0%6.7%

Est. 1328(f) Violations7213,856

What This Means

A high dismissal rate means that most debtors who enter Chapter 13 in this district do not complete
their repayment plans. Dismissal can occur for many reasons -- missed payments, failure to file required
documents, inability to propose a confirmable plan, or voluntary dismissal by the debtor.

An estimated 72 discharges in this district may have been granted in violation of
the Section 1328(f) waiting period between 2015 and 2024. No federal court currently screens for
these violations automatically. The Open Bankruptcy Project's free Discharge Eligibility Screener is the
only public tool available to check 1328(f) eligibility.

Related Resources

Dismissal Rate Data -- interactive dismissal rate data for all 94 districts

Discharge Eligibility Screener -- check bankruptcy discharge eligibility under Section 1328(f)

Where Chapter 13 Fails: The 15 Worst Districts

Repeat Bankruptcy Filers by District

Methodology: Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (FJC IDB),
covering Chapter 13 cases filed in federal fiscal years 2020-2024. Dismissal and discharge rates are calculated
among cases with known dispositions. Prior filer rates reflect cases where a prior bankruptcy filing was identified
in the FJC record. Estimated 1328(f) violations are modeled based on discharge-despite-prior-filing patterns and
statutory waiting periods. This analysis was conducted by the Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team.

36.6%

4.8%

documents, inability to propose a confirmable plan, or voluntary dismissal by the debtor.

dismissal rates in the country, well below the national average of 75.6%.

## Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in D. Vermont: 258 Cases, 53.8% Dismissal Rate -- Open Bankruptcy Project
Source: https://openbankruptcyproject.org/news/chapter-13-d-vermont-bankruptcy-data.html

Open Bankruptcy Project

News
Research
About
Contact

KANSAS CITY, April 3, 2026 --

Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in D. Vermont: 258 Cases, 53.8% Dismissal Rate

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

258

Ch.13 Cases Filed

53.8%

Dismissal Rate

12.0%

Prior Filer Rate

6.2%

Pro Se Rate

The D. Vermont in Vermont processed 258 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years
2020 and 2024, according to data from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database.
Of cases with known outcomes, 53.8% were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --
ranking this district #84 out of 91 measured districts nationally.

The national Chapter 13 dismissal rate for the same period is 75.6%, making D. Vermont's
rate far below the national average.

Key Statistics

MetricD. VermontNational

Ch.13 Cases Filed (FY2020-2024)258834,483

Dismissal Rate53.8%75.6%

Prior Filer Rate12.0%36.6%

Prior Filer Discharge RateN/A15.1%

Pro Se Rate6.2%6.7%

Est. 1328(f) ViolationsN/A13,856

What This Means

A high dismissal rate means that most debtors who enter Chapter 13 in this district do not complete
their repayment plans. Dismissal can occur for many reasons -- missed payments, failure to file required
documents, inability to propose a confirmable plan, or voluntary dismissal by the debtor.

Dismissal rate: 53.8% -- D. Vermont has one of the lowest Chapter 13
dismissal rates in the country, well below the national average of 75.6%.

Related Resources

Dismissal Rate Data -- interactive dismissal rate data for all 94 districts

Discharge Eligibility Screener -- check bankruptcy discharge eligibility under Section 1328(f)

Where Chapter 13 Fails: The 15 Worst Districts

Repeat Bankruptcy Filers by District

Methodology: Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (FJC IDB),
covering Chapter 13 cases filed in federal fiscal years 2020-2024. Dismissal and discharge rates are calculated
among cases with known dispositions. Prior filer rates reflect cases where a prior bankruptcy filing was identified
in the FJC record. Estimated 1328(f) violations are modeled based on discharge-despite-prior-filing patterns and
statutory waiting periods. This analysis was conducted by the Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team.

Free, open-source bankruptcy transparency. No ads. No affiliate links. Supported by donations.

© 2026 Open Bankruptcy Project · 501(c)(3) Nonprofit ·

Privacy · Terms ·

No legal advice is provided. This is educational information only.

This site provides general information, not legal advice. Consult a qualified attorney for your specific situation.

1099-C Cancellation of Debt Tax Guide -- 1099cdebt.com

## Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in E.D. Wash.: 1,773 Cases, 53.1% Dismissal Rate -- Open Bankruptcy Project
Source: https://openbankruptcyproject.org/news/chapter-13-e-d-wash-bankruptcy-data.html

Open Bankruptcy Project

News
Research
About
Contact

KANSAS CITY, April 3, 2026 --

Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in E.D. Wash.: 1,773 Cases, 53.1% Dismissal Rate

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

1,773

Ch.13 Cases Filed

53.1%

Dismissal Rate

30.2%

Prior Filer Rate

7.4%

Pro Se Rate

The E.D. Wash. in Washington processed 1,773 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years
2020 and 2024, according to data from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database.
Of cases with known outcomes, 53.1% were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --
ranking this district #85 out of 91 measured districts nationally.

The national Chapter 13 dismissal rate for the same period is 75.6%, making E.D. Wash.'s
rate far below the national average.

Key Statistics

MetricE.D. Wash.National

Ch.13 Cases Filed (FY2020-2024)1,773834,483

Dismissal Rate53.1%75.6%

Prior Filer Rate30.2%36.6%

Prior Filer Discharge Rate34.8%15.1%

Pro Se Rate7.4%6.7%

Est. 1328(f) Violations5213,856

What This Means

A high dismissal rate means that most debtors who enter Chapter 13 in this district do not complete
their repayment plans. Dismissal can occur for many reasons -- missed payments, failure to file required
documents, inability to propose a confirmable plan, or voluntary dismissal by the debtor.

Dismissal rate: 53.1% -- E.D. Wash. has one of the lowest Chapter 13
dismissal rates in the country, well below the national average of 75.6%.

An estimated 52 discharges in this district may have been granted in violation of
the Section 1328(f) waiting period between 2015 and 2024. No federal court currently screens for
these violations automatically. The Open Bankruptcy Project's free Discharge Eligibility Screener is the
only public tool available to check 1328(f) eligibility.

Related Resources

Dismissal Rate Data -- interactive dismissal rate data for all 94 districts

Discharge Eligibility Screener -- check bankruptcy discharge eligibility under Section 1328(f)

Where Chapter 13 Fails: The 15 Worst Districts

Repeat Bankruptcy Filers by District

Methodology: Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (FJC IDB),
covering Chapter 13 cases filed in federal fiscal years 2020-2024. Dismissal and discharge rates are calculated
among cases with known dispositions. Prior filer rates reflect cases where a prior bankruptcy filing was identified
in the FJC record. Estimated 1328(f) violations are modeled based on discharge-despite-prior-filing patterns and
statutory waiting periods. This analysis was conducted by the Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team.

Published April 3, 2026

Ch.13 Cases Filed

53.0%

Dismissal Rate

17.4%

Prior Filer Rate

2.8%

Pro Se Rate

The D. Hawaii in Hawaii processed 1,867 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years

Of cases with known outcomes, 53.0% were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --
ranking this district #86 out of 91 measured districts nationally.

Dismissal rate: 53.0% -- D. Hawaii has one of the lowest Chapter 13
dismissal rates in the country, well below the national average of 75.6%.

Ch.13 Cases Filed

28.6%

1.2%

Ch.13 Cases Filed

37.7%

2.8%

Ch.13 Cases Filed

51.4%

29.1%

1.8%

## Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in D.V.I.: 6 Cases, 0 Dismissal Rate -- Open Bankruptcy Project
Source: https://openbankruptcyproject.org/news/chapter-13-d-v-i-bankruptcy-data.html

Open Bankruptcy Project

News
Research
About
Contact

KANSAS CITY, April 3, 2026 --

Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in D.V.I.: 6 Cases, 0 Dismissal Rate

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

6

Ch.13 Cases Filed

0

Dismissal Rate

16.7%

Prior Filer Rate

83.3%

Pro Se Rate

The D.V.I. in Virgin Islands processed 6 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years
2020 and 2024, according to data from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database.
Of cases with known outcomes, 0 were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --
ranking this district #92 out of 91 measured districts nationally.

The national Chapter 13 dismissal rate for the same period is 75.6%, making D.V.I.'s
rate far below the national average.

Key Statistics

MetricD.V.I.National

Ch.13 Cases Filed (FY2020-2024)6834,483

Dismissal Rate075.6%

Prior Filer Rate16.7%36.6%

Prior Filer Discharge RateN/A15.1%

Pro Se Rate83.3%6.7%

Est. 1328(f) ViolationsN/A13,856

What This Means

A high dismissal rate means that most debtors who enter Chapter 13 in this district do not complete
their repayment plans. Dismissal can occur for many reasons -- missed payments, failure to file required
documents, inability to propose a confirmable plan, or voluntary dismissal by the debtor.

Dismissal rate: 0 -- D.V.I. has one of the lowest Chapter 13
dismissal rates in the country, well below the national average of 75.6%.

The pro se rate of 83.3% is notably high. Debtors without attorney representation face
significantly higher dismissal rates in Chapter 13, where the procedural requirements for plan
confirmation are complex. See Pro Se Bankruptcy Guide for more data.

Related Resources

Dismissal Rate Data -- interactive dismissal rate data for all 94 districts

Discharge Eligibility Screener -- check bankruptcy discharge eligibility under Section 1328(f)

Where Chapter 13 Fails: The 15 Worst Districts

Repeat Bankruptcy Filers by District

Methodology: Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (FJC IDB),
covering Chapter 13 cases filed in federal fiscal years 2020-2024. Dismissal and discharge rates are calculated
among cases with known dispositions. Prior filer rates reflect cases where a prior bankruptcy filing was identified
in the FJC record. Estimated 1328(f) violations are modeled based on discharge-despite-prior-filing patterns and
statutory waiting periods. This analysis was conducted by the Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team.

## Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in D. Guam: 50 Cases, 0 Dismissal Rate -- Open Bankruptcy Project
Source: https://openbankruptcyproject.org/news/chapter-13-d-guam-bankruptcy-data.html

Open Bankruptcy Project

News
Research
About
Contact

KANSAS CITY, April 3, 2026 --

Chapter 13 Bankruptcy in D. Guam: 50 Cases, 0 Dismissal Rate

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

50

Ch.13 Cases Filed

0

Dismissal Rate

22.0%

Prior Filer Rate

10.0%

Pro Se Rate

The D. Guam in Guam processed 50 Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings between fiscal years
2020 and 2024, according to data from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database.
Of cases with known outcomes, 0 were dismissed before the debtor received a discharge --
ranking this district #93 out of 91 measured districts nationally.

The national Chapter 13 dismissal rate for the same period is 75.6%, making D. Guam's
rate far below the national average.

Key Statistics

MetricD. GuamNational

Ch.13 Cases Filed (FY2020-2024)50834,483

Dismissal Rate075.6%

Prior Filer Rate22.0%36.6%

Prior Filer Discharge RateN/A15.1%

Pro Se Rate10.0%6.7%

Est. 1328(f) ViolationsN/A13,856

What This Means

A high dismissal rate means that most debtors who enter Chapter 13 in this district do not complete
their repayment plans. Dismissal can occur for many reasons -- missed payments, failure to file required
documents, inability to propose a confirmable plan, or voluntary dismissal by the debtor.

Dismissal rate: 0 -- D. Guam has one of the lowest Chapter 13
dismissal rates in the country, well below the national average of 75.6%.

Related Resources

Dismissal Rate Data -- interactive dismissal rate data for all 94 districts

Discharge Eligibility Screener -- check bankruptcy discharge eligibility under Section 1328(f)

Where Chapter 13 Fails: The 15 Worst Districts

Repeat Bankruptcy Filers by District

Methodology: Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (FJC IDB),
covering Chapter 13 cases filed in federal fiscal years 2020-2024. Dismissal and discharge rates are calculated
among cases with known dispositions. Prior filer rates reflect cases where a prior bankruptcy filing was identified
in the FJC record. Estimated 1328(f) violations are modeled based on discharge-despite-prior-filing patterns and
statutory waiting periods. This analysis was conducted by the Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team.

Free, open-source bankruptcy transparency. No ads. No affiliate links. Supported by donations.

## Bankruptcy in New York: 24,539 Chapter 13 Cases Across 4 Districts -- Open Bankruptcy Project
Source: https://openbankruptcyproject.org/news/bankruptcy-statistics-new-york-2024.html

Open Bankruptcy Project

News
Research
About
Contact

KANSAS CITY, April 3, 2026 --

Bankruptcy in New York: 24,539 Chapter 13 Cases Across 4 Districts

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

24,539

Total Ch.13 Filings

85.1%

Avg Dismissal Rate

4

Federal Districts

189

Est. 1328(f) Violations

New York is served by 4 federal bankruptcy districts.
Between fiscal years 2020 and 2024, 24,539 Chapter 13 cases were filed statewide,
with a weighted average dismissal rate of 85.1% -- compared to the national average
of 75.6%.

District-by-District Breakdown

DistrictFiledDismiss %Prior %Pro Se %Est. Violations

E.D.N.Y.13,18796.4%53.5%58.2%50

S.D.N.Y.4,26380.0%36.8%20.8%51

N.D.N.Y.4,03460.0%25.6%4.1%66

W.D.N.Y.3,05576.8%25.5%8.1%22

36.4 percentage point spread between New York's best and worst districts.
N.D.N.Y. dismisses 60.0% of cases while E.D.N.Y.
dismisses 96.4% -- same state law, dramatically different outcomes.

Related Resources

Dismissal Rate Data -- compare all 94 districts

Discharge Eligibility Screener -- check 1328(f) discharge eligibility

Exemptions by State -- New York bankruptcy exemptions

All OBP News & Analysis

Methodology: Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (FJC IDB),
covering Chapter 13 cases filed in federal fiscal years 2020-2024. Weighted averages are calculated using
filing volume as weights. This analysis was conducted by the Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team.

## Bankruptcy in Kentucky: 20,892 Chapter 13 Cases Across 2 Districts -- Open Bankruptcy Project
Source: https://openbankruptcyproject.org/news/bankruptcy-statistics-kentucky-2024.html

Open Bankruptcy Project

News
Research
About
Contact

KANSAS CITY, April 3, 2026 --

Bankruptcy in Kentucky: 20,892 Chapter 13 Cases Across 2 Districts

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

20,892

Total Ch.13 Filings

67.6%

Avg Dismissal Rate

2

Federal Districts

352

Est. 1328(f) Violations

Kentucky is served by 2 federal bankruptcy districts.
Between fiscal years 2020 and 2024, 20,892 Chapter 13 cases were filed statewide,
with a weighted average dismissal rate of 67.6% -- compared to the national average
of 75.6%.

District-by-District Breakdown

DistrictFiledDismiss %Prior %Pro Se %Est. Violations

W.D. Ky.12,75877.4%30.8%1.2%160

E.D. Ky.8,13452.2%28.6%1.2%192

25.2 percentage point spread between Kentucky's best and worst districts.
E.D. Ky. dismisses 52.2% of cases while W.D. Ky.
dismisses 77.4% -- same state law, dramatically different outcomes.

Related Resources

Dismissal Rate Data -- compare all 94 districts

Discharge Eligibility Screener -- check 1328(f) discharge eligibility

filing volume as weights. This analysis was conducted by the Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team.

Published April 3, 2026

27,400

Total Ch.13 Filings

71.1%

Avg Dismissal Rate

2

Federal Districts

537

Est. 1328(f) Violations

Virginia is served by 2 federal bankruptcy districts.

with a weighted average dismissal rate of 71.1% -- compared to the national average
of 75.6%.

DistrictFiledDismiss %Prior %Pro Se %Est. Violations

E.D. Va.21,23176.1%38.8%5.4%343

W.D. Va.6,16954.0%36.6%4.8%194

22.1 percentage point spread between Virginia's best and worst districts.

dismisses 76.1% -- same state law, dramatically different outcomes.

Bankruptcy in West Virginia: 1,223 Chapter 13 Cases Across 2 Districts

By Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team

Published April 3, 2026

1,223

Total Ch.13 Filings

64.4%

Avg Dismissal Rate

2

Federal Districts

20

Est. 1328(f) Violations

West Virginia is served by 2 federal bankruptcy districts.
Between fiscal years 2020 and 2024, 1,223 Chapter 13 cases were filed statewide,
with a weighted average dismissal rate of 64.4% -- compared to the national average
of 75.6%.

District-by-District Breakdown

DistrictFiledDismiss %Prior %Pro Se %Est. Violations

N.D.W. Va.62862.1%23.6%12.7%11

S.D.W. Va.59566.8%25.5%7.4%9

Related Resources

Dismissal Rate Data -- compare all 94 districts

Discharge Eligibility Screener -- check 1328(f) discharge eligibility

Exemptions by State -- West Virginia bankruptcy exemptions

All OBP News & Analysis

Methodology: Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (FJC IDB),
covering Chapter 13 cases filed in federal fiscal years 2020-2024. Weighted averages are calculated using
filing volume as weights. This analysis was conducted by the Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team.

Free, open-source bankruptcy transparency. No ads. No affiliate links. Supported by donations.

Published April 3, 2026

7,972

Total Ch.13 Filings

57.4%

Avg Dismissal Rate

2

Federal Districts

218

Est. 1328(f) Violations

Washington is served by 2 federal bankruptcy districts.
Between fiscal years 2020 and 2024, 7,972 Chapter 13 cases were filed statewide,
with a weighted average dismissal rate of 57.4% -- compared to the national average
of 75.6%.

## Bankruptcy by City -- Local Guides for Every Metro
Source: https://openbankruptcyproject.org/cities.html

Open Bankruptcy Project

Mission
Network
Cities
News
Research
About
Contact

Bankruptcy by City

Find bankruptcy information specific to your city. Real data from the Federal Judicial Center covering 4.9 million cases across all 94 federal districts.

Sorted by Chapter 13 dismissal rate -- worst first.

79.6%
Worst Dismissal Rate

14
Metro Areas Covered

1,182,837
Total Cases Analyzed

35.4%
Best Dismissal Rate

City Directory

Every guide includes local dismissal rates, district-specific data, filing information, and educational resources. All free, all backed by federal court data.

#1 Highest
Memphis
W.D. Tenn.

79.6%
Dismissal Rate

173,823
Total Cases

memphisbankruptcy.org

#2
Atlanta

How We Calculate Dismissal Rates

All data comes from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database (FJC IDB), which contains records for every federal bankruptcy case filed in the United States. The Open Bankruptcy Project screens these records to calculate district-level dismissal rates for Chapter 13 cases.

A "dismissal" means the case was terminated without the debtor receiving a discharge -- the legal order that eliminates qualifying debts. High dismissal rates often indicate structural issues in how cases are filed, managed, or administered in a district.

Each city guide is mapped to its primary federal judicial district. Some metro areas span multiple districts; we use the district where the city's federal courthouse is located. Case counts reflect all bankruptcy filings in the district, not just the named city.

Frequently Asked Questions

Which city has the highest bankruptcy dismissal rate?

Memphis, Tennessee has the highest Chapter 13 bankruptcy dismissal rate among major U.S. metros at 79.6%, based on Federal Judicial Center data covering 173,823 cases in the Western District of Tennessee. Nearly 4 out of 5 Chapter 13 cases filed in Memphis end in dismissal rather than discharge.

Why do bankruptcy dismissal rates vary so much by city?

Dismissal rates vary by city due to differences in local court culture, attorney practice patterns, debtor demographics, plan feasibility standards, and trustee oversight intensity. Some districts have structural issues with high-volume practices that file cases unlikely to succeed, inflating dismissal rates.

What does a high dismissal rate mean for bankruptcy filers?

A high dismissal rate means a large proportion of bankruptcy cases end without the debtor receiving a discharge -- the legal order that eliminates qualifying debts. Dismissed cases leave debtors with no relief, potential refiling bars under 11 U.S.C. Section 109(g), and often thousands of dollars in attorney fees already paid.

Where does the bankruptcy city data come from?

All data comes from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database, which contains records for every federal bankruptcy case filed in the United States. The Open Bankruptcy Project has screened 4.9 million cases across all 94 federal judicial districts.

How can I find bankruptcy information for my city?

The Open Bankruptcy Project operates local bankruptcy guides for 14 major metro areas. Each guide includes local dismissal rates, district-specific data, filing information, and educational resources. Click any city card above to visit the local guide for that metro area.

Check Your Discharge Eligibility

If you have filed bankruptcy before, check whether you are eligible to receive a discharge in a new case. Free, instant, no signup required.

About Homestead Exemptions

A homestead exemption determines how much equity in your primary residence is protected from creditors in bankruptcy. Each state sets its own exemption amount. Some states -- like Iowa and Arkansas -- offer unlimited protection, meaning no creditor can force the sale of your home regardless of its value.

States with low homestead exemptions (like Tennessee at $5,000 or Illinois and Missouri at $15,000) leave homeowners with significant equity at risk in Chapter 7 bankruptcy. In these states, a trustee could sell your home if your equity exceeds the exempt amount.

The number of federal judicial districts in a state affects where you file and which local rules apply. States with multiple districts -- like Georgia (3), Tennessee (3), and Ohio (2) -- can have dramatically different court cultures, trustee practices, and dismissal rates depending on which district handles your case.

Frequently Asked Questions

Which states have unlimited homestead exemptions in bankruptcy?

Iowa and Arkansas offer unlimited homestead exemptions in bankruptcy. In Iowa, the homestead exemption has no dollar cap regardless of property value. In Arkansas, the exemption is unlimited for married filers or heads of household. These states allow debtors to protect their primary residence from creditors in bankruptcy regardless of the home's value, though federal exemptions may apply differently.

Which state has the highest Chapter 13 dismissal rate?

Tennessee has the highest Chapter 13 dismissal rate in our state guide network, with Memphis (Western District of Tennessee) reaching 79.6% based on Federal Judicial Center data. Georgia follows closely, with the Northern District of Georgia (Atlanta) at 72.9%. Both states have three federal judicial districts each.

How many federal judicial districts does each state have?

The number of federal judicial districts varies by state. Among the 14 states in our guide network: Georgia, Illinois, and Tennessee each have 3 districts. Arkansas, Iowa, Missouri, and Ohio each have 2 districts. Alaska, Colorado, Delaware, Maryland, Minnesota, Rhode Island, and Vermont each have 1 district. More districts generally means more variation in local court practices.

What is a homestead exemption in bankruptcy?

A homestead exemption protects equity in your primary residence from creditors during bankruptcy. Each state sets its own exemption amount -- ranging from as low as $5,000 in Tennessee to unlimited in Iowa and Arkansas. The exemption determines how much home equity you can keep. If your equity exceeds the exemption, a Chapter 7 trustee could sell the home and pay you the exempt amount. States with higher exemptions offer more protection for homeowners filing bankruptcy.

Where does the state bankruptcy data come from?

All data comes from the Federal Judicial Center's Integrated Database (FJC IDB), which contains records for every federal bankruptcy case filed in the United States. The Open Bankruptcy Project has screened 4.9 million cases across all 94 federal judicial districts. Homestead exemption amounts are based on current state statutes and are updated periodically.
